reasoning: Martinique's 360-day average of 3.167 protests per 30-day period is likely inflated by the intense wave of protests over the cost of living that peaked in late 2024. Protest movements of this nature typically exhibit substantial mean-reverting behavior over a timeframe of an entire year. Thus, the baseline rate (lambda) of protests in late 2025 and early 2026 is expected to drop below the historic 360-day average as the crisis cools. Furthermore, even if the protest rate had remained perfectly stationary at a constant lambda of 3.167, the Poisson probability of observing strictly more than 3.167 protests (i.e., 4 or more protests) in a single given 30-day window is only about 39%. Given our expected decrease in protest frequency, the true probability is likely significantly lower, in the 15-25% range. Following the forecasting guidelines, uncertainty increases over the forecast horizon, pulling the estimates for more distant dates closer to 0.5.
